key_insights.insight: Reading non-verbal communication is a form of intelligence that relies on empathy and attunement to others' emotions, not algorithms.
key_insights.quote: In reading non-verbal communication it's a different form of intelligence. It's not algorithms. It's not formulaic. It's human. It's emotional. It's empathetic.

key_insights.insight: The eyes are difficult to lie with, and even skilled actors struggle to truly fake genuine eye contact.
key_insights.quote: Eyes are hard to lie. They tell you something. Now, actors, very skilled actors, can kind of create some of these impressions.

key_insights.insight: The tone of voice is a strong indicator of confidence levels and emotional state, and is very difficult to consciously manipulate.
key_insights.quote: So the voice of a person tells you a lot about their confidence levels, tells you a lot about, you know, how just about their general emotional tone, about their character, about who they are. It's very difficult to fake.

key_insights.insight: Genuine smiles engage the whole face, not just the mouth, and reveal true joy.
key_insights.quote: The authentic smile... lights up the whole face, you know, like you really feel joy... your eyes go up your cheeks go up everything kind of connects together.

key_insights.insight: Observe someone's body language when they are surprised to see if their initial reaction reveals their true feelings.
key_insights.quote: When you catch people by surprise and they don't have the time to put on a fake smile... for a second, you detect what we call a micro expression of disdain... They can't fake it.

key_insights.insight: Surrounding yourself with positive, accomplished individuals can 'infect' you with their positive energy and drive.
key_insights.quote: Being around people who do things, who get things done, who've made a business, who've made this, that, or the other, their goal to be around, because they'll infect you with their positive energy.

key_insights.insight: Be wary of individuals who consistently portray themselves as victims, as their negativity can be contagious and detrimental.
key_insights.quote: You have to avoid people like that. You have to read before you get involved with them that they are a drama queen or a drama king because there are just as many men out there who have this quality. You have to see that they play the victim of everybody else.

actionable_advice.advice: To assess someone's true feelings, approach them from an angle to catch their unguarded microexpression.
actionable_advice.how_to: Surprise them and observe their initial facial reaction before they have time to put on a fake smile. Look for microexpressions of disdain.

actionable_advice.advice: Evaluate the tone of someone's voice for hesitations or inconsistencies to detect potential deception or lack of confidence.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pay close attention to the voice's cadence, speed, and presence of stammering or hesitations, as these are difficult to consciously control.

actionable_advice.advice: Be intentional about the people you surround yourself with and choose those who are confident and accomplished.
actionable_advice.how_to: Seek out individuals who are proactive, driven, and have a positive impact, as their energy can be infectious and beneficial.

memorable_stories: Anecdote about befriending a dramatic person and slowly being infected by their negativity.
contrarian_claims: That simply being around other people is infectious, whether it's good or bad.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 172: 5 Body Language Rules To Read Anyone INSTANTLY!: Robert Greene
